message.content:
Read all specification files in specs/ directory systematically. For each spec, extract:
1. Key requirements and features
2. Technical specifications
3. Dependencies on other components
4. Critical constraints

Files to read:
- specs/001-executive-summary.md
- specs/002-architecture.md
- specs/003-database-schema.md
- specs/004-domain-services.md
- specs/005-api.md
- specs/006-user-interface.md
- specs/007-integration.md
- specs/008-operations.md

Provide a comprehensive summary of all specifications organized by domain.

content.text: I'll systematically read all specification files to extract requirements, technical specs, dependencies, and constraints. Let me start by reading all the spec files.

content.content:
1â†’# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
     2â†’# Part 1: Executive Summary & Decision Record
     3â†’
     4â†’**Document Version:** 2.1  
     5â†’**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
     6â†’**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19
     7â†’
     8â†’---
     9â†’
    10â†’## 1.1 System Purpose
    11â†’
    12â†’The Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS) is a constraint-based fleet management platform that optimizes train deployments and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year contract period. It replaces manual Excel-based planning with an integrated system providing:
    13â†’
    14â†’1. **Daily mileage tracking** with automated ingestion from CSV uploads
    15â†’2. **Run assignment optimization** balancing mileage across the fleet
    16â†’3. **Preventive maintenance scheduling** within resource constraints
    17â†’4. **Strategic planning** for 1-10 year horizons to prevent "bow wave" accumulation
    18â†’5. **Man-hour leveling** to optimize workforce utilization
    19â†’6. **Cost forecasting** with TCO projection and variance analysis
    20â†’7. **Simulation capabilities** for what-if scenario planning
    21â†’8. **Unified visualization** via an Infinite Canvas interface
    22â†’9. **Service demand validation** ensuring fleet availability meets operational requirements
    23â†’10. **Unavailability awareness** integrating corrective maintenance status for schedule validity
    24â†’
    25â†’---
    26â†’
    27â†’## 1.2 Project Scope
    28â†’
    29â†’### 1.2.1 In Scope (MMS Manages)
    30â†’
    31â†’| Capability | Description |
    32â†’|------------|-------------|
    33â†’| Fleet roster management | Trains, cars, components with lifecycle tracking |
    34â†’| Preventive Maintenance scheduling | C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul planning with constraint validation |
    35â†’| Run assignment optimization | Daily route allocation with mileage balancing |
    36â†’| Mileage projection and tracking | Route-based utilization profiles, 12-year forecasting |
    37â†’| Constraint validation | Track capacity, labor, materials, unavailability |
    38â†’| Maximo integration | Bidirectional sync for PM work orders |
    39â†’| Multi-year capital planning | Bow-wave detection, cost smoothing |
    40â†’| Service demand validation | Ensuring minimum fleet availability targets |
    41â†’
    42â†’### 1.2.2 In Scope (MMS Must Receive/React To)
    43â†’
    44â†’| External Data | Source | Purpose |
    45â†’|---------------|--------|---------|
    46â†’| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook/polling | Block unavailable trains from scheduling |
    47â†’| Inventory snapshots | ERP/Maximo | Validate material constraints |
    48â†’| Service demand requirements | Operations team | Set minimum availability targets |
    49â†’
    50â†’### 1.2.3 Out of Scope (MMS Does Not Execute)
    51â†’
    52â†’| Capability | Owner System | MMS Interaction |
    53â†’|------------|--------------|-----------------|
    54â†’| Corrective maintenance work orders | Maximo CMMS | Receives status updates only |
    55â†’| Parts inventory management | ERP/Maximo | Consumes snapshots for constraint validation |
    56â†’| Operator scheduling | Workforce management | None |
    57â†’| Real-time GPS tracking | OCC systems | None |
    58â†’
    59â†’> **Critical Clarification:** "Out of Scope" means MMS does not *manage* these functions. However, MMS **must receive** corrective maintenance status to prevent scheduling conflicts. Without this visibility, every PM schedule is potentially invalid on the day of execution.
    60â†’
    61â†’---
    62â†’
    63â†’## 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions
    64â†’
    65â†’| ID | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered | Risk Mitigation |
    66â†’|----|----------|-----------|------------------------|-----------------|
    67â†’| ADR-001 | TypeScript-First Architecture | Team expertise, single language for 98% of codebase, strong typing | Java (enterprise), Python (data science) | Node.js fallback path documented |
    68â†’| ADR-002 | C++ Native Binary for Tactical Optimization | Container density (20-30 pods/node), <5ms cold start, OR-Tools native performance | WASM solver (limited), Pure TS (10x slower) | TypeScript fallback solver, Docker build pipeline |
    69â†’| ADR-003 | Dual-Horizon Planning (Strategic + Tactical) | RFQ requires 1-10 year planning; single solver cannot handle both granularities | Single solver (bow wave risk), Manual strategic planning | Constraint aggregation layer ensures consistency |
    70â†’| ADR-004 | Soft Constraints with Explainability | Operations cannot accept "INFEASIBLE"; must provide actionable least-bad solutions | Hard constraints only (operationally unacceptable) | Fallback greedy solver, explanation trace |
    71â†’| ADR-005 | Material Constraints in Optimizer | PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless | Labor-only optimization (cited in reviews as critical gap) | Inventory snapshot integration, reservation system |
    72â†’| ADR-006 | Baseline/Drift Architecture | Long-term plans cannot recalculate daily; planners need stable reference points | Continuous recalculation (jittery UX, trust erosion) | Published baselines, drift thresholds, dampening |
    73â†’| ADR-007 | Scenario-Based Simulation | RFQ requires what-if analysis; cannot store in single-reality schema | In-memory only (data loss), Full DB clone (storage bloat) | Event-sourced deltas, materialized comparison views |
    74â†’| ADR-008 | Infinite Canvas UI (deck.gl) | Semantic zoom from 12-year strategy to daily operations without context switching | Paginated tables (poor UX), Separate views (fragmented) | Progressive enhancement, tabular fallback |
    75â†’| ADR-009 | Explanation Trace in Optimizer Output | Operators must understand "why" to trust and adopt system recommendations | Black-box output (user rejection, manual overrides) | Factor-based explanations, human-readable descriptions |
    76â†’| ADR-010 | Audit Trail for All Schedule Changes | 12-year government contract requires full attribution and compliance evidence | Implicit logging only | Immutable history tables, user attribution |
    77â†’| **ADR-011** | **Unavailability Event Integration** | PM scheduling requires knowledge of trains blocked by corrective work; without this, schedules are invalid on day of execution | Ignore corrective (operationally invalid) | Maximo webhook integration; manual status update UI as fallback |
    78â†’| **ADR-012** | **Service Demand as Hard Constraint** | Optimizer must know minimum fleet availability target; cannot validate bow-wave risk without knowing "how many trains do we need?" | Assume 100% availability (unrealistic) | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown |
    79â†’| **ADR-013** | **Route-Based Utilization Profiles** | Mileage projection accuracy requires route-specific daily rates, not fleet averages; projection error compounds over 12-year horizon | Fleet-wide average (60,000 km error over 12 years) | Route utilization profiles table; manual override per train; explicit flags for unknown routes |
    80â†’| **ADR-014** | **PM History Assumptions for Cold Start** | Data migration must handle incomplete historical records with explicit confidence levels; system must not crash or produce invalid projections from missing data | Assume complete history (crash risk), Ignore missing (false alerts) | Assumption tracking table; inference rules; confidence scoring; manual review queue |
    81â†’
    82â†’---
    83â†’
    84â†’## 1.4 Risk Assessment & Mitigation
    85â†’
    86â†’| Risk ID | Risk Description | Probability | Impact | Mitigation Strategy | Owner |
    87â†’|---------|------------------|-------------|--------|---------------------|-------|
    88â†’| R-001 | C++ binary build complexity | Medium | High | Containerized build pipeline, pre-built binaries, TypeScript fallback | DevOps |
    89â†’| R-002 | Team TypeScript focus with C++ component | Low | Medium | C++ contractor with knowledge transfer, comprehensive integration tests | Tech Lead |
    90â†’| R-003 | Strategic/Tactical solver inconsistency | Medium | High | Constraint aggregation layer, cross-validation tests, unified constraint model | Architect |
    91â†’| R-004 | Material constraint data quality | High | High | Inventory snapshot validation, fallback to labor-only mode, data quality dashboard | Data Team |
    92â†’| R-005 | Operator distrust of optimizer | Medium | High | Explanation traces, gradual rollout, manual override capability, training program | Product |
    93â†’| R-006 | Long-term plan instability (jitter) | Medium | Medium | Baseline snapshots, drift thresholds, dampening in projections | Backend |
    94â†’| R-007 | deck.gl WebGL performance on low-end devices | Medium | Medium | Progressive enhancement, LOD rendering, tabular fallback | Frontend |
    95â†’| R-008 | Data migration quality issues | Medium | High | Parallel-run validation, automated reconciliation, rollback procedures | Data Team |
    96â†’| R-009 | Scenario storage scalability | Low | Medium | Event-sourced deltas vs. full clones, automatic scenario expiration | Backend |
    97â†’| R-010 | Maximo integration delays | Medium | Medium | CSV interim solution, shadow record system, dedicated integration team | Integration |
    98â†’| **R-011** | **No corrective maintenance visibility** | **High** | **Critical** | Unavailability event feed from Maximo; fallback to manual status update UI; alert when train scheduled without availability check | Integration |
    99â†’| **R-012** | **Service demand undefined** | **High** | **Critical** | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown; prominent warning in UI when using defaults | Product |
   100â†’| **R-013** | **PM history incomplete at migration** | **High** | **High** | Assumption tracking table (`pm_baseline_assumptions`); inference rules from higher-level PMs; confidence scoring; manual review queue for LOW confidence trains | Data Team |
   101â†’| **R-014** | **Route utilization unknown** | **Medium** | **High** | Default to fleet-wide average with explicit warning flag; allow manual override per train; route profile editor in admin UI | Product |
   102â†’
   103â†’---
   104â†’
   105â†’## 1.5 RFQ Compliance Matrix
   106â†’
   107â†’| RFQ Requirement | Status | Implementation Section | Notes |
   108â†’|-----------------|--------|----------------------|-------|

   111â†’| Run assignment optimization | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 4: Run Assignment Optimizer | C++ CP-SAT with soft constraints |
   112â†’| PM scheduling (weekly/monthly) | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 4: PM Scheduler | 12-week tactical horizon |
   113â†’| Long-term planning (1-10 years) | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Genetic algorithm with constraint envelope |
   114â†’| Man-hour projection & leveling | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 4: Man-hour Leveling Engine | Multi-strategy adjustments |
   115â†’| Spare parts availability | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 3: Material Constraints Schema | Inventory integration, reservation system |
   116â†’| Costing module (TCO, variance) | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 4: Costing Service | Labor rates, material costs, shop rates |
   117â†’| Simulation engine | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 4: Simulation Service | Scenario branching, comparison views |
   118â†’| Guidance and warnings | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 4: Explanation Trace | Factor-based assignment explanations |
   119â†’| Maximo integration | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 7: Integration | Phased: CSV أ¢â€ â€™ API أ¢â€ â€™ Bidirectional |
   120â†’| Web-based UI (desktop focus) | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 6: User Interface | Infinite Canvas with semantic zoom |
   121â†’| Email notifications & reports | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 5: API Specification | Notification service, PDF/Excel export |
   122â†’| 12-year contract support | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Baseline/drift architecture |
   123â†’| Strategic simulation capability | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 4: Simulation Service | What-if scenarios with persistence |
   124â†’| Component-level tracking | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 3: Component Tracking Schema | Mileage follows components |
   125â†’| Data migration | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 8: Data Migration | ETL pipeline, validation suite |
   126â†’| Documentation deliverables | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 8: Documentation Plan | User manual, API reference, training |
   127â†’| **Service availability validation** | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 3: Service Demand Schema | Min train requirements by day type |
   128â†’| **Corrective maintenance awareness** | أ¢إ“â€¦ | Part 7: CM Integration | Maximo unavailability feed |
   129â†’
   130â†’---
   131â†’

   164â†’
   165â†’---
   166â†’
   167â†’## 1.7 Glossary of Terms
   168â†’
   169â†’| Term | Definition |
   170â†’|------|------------|
   171â†’| PM | Preventive Maintenance (C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul) |
   172â†’| Fixed Parameters | Resource constraints (tracks, man-hours) that bound optimization |
   173â†’| Run Assignment | Allocation of a specific train to a specific route for a day |
   174â†’| Mileage Leveling | Balancing cumulative mileage across the fleet to equalize wear |
   175â†’| Man-hour Leveling | Balancing workforce allocation across time periods |
   176â†’| Compliance Window | Acceptable date range for scheduling a PM (earliest to latest) |
   177â†’| Bow Wave Risk | Accumulation of deferred maintenance causing future overload |
   178â†’| Soft Constraint | Optimization constraint that can be violated with penalty cost |
   179â†’| Hard Constraint | Constraint that must never be violated (safety, physical limits) |
   180â†’| Strategic Horizon | Long-term planning window (1-10 years, quarterly granularity) |
   181â†’| Tactical Horizon | Medium-term planning window (12 weeks, daily granularity) |
   182â†’| Baseline | Published snapshot of a plan used as reference for drift analysis |
   183â†’| Drift | Deviation between actual state and baseline projection |
   184â†’| Scenario | Alternative version of a plan for what-if analysis |
   185â†’| Explanation Trace | Human-readable justification for optimizer decisions |
   186â†’| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership (12-year projected costs) |
   187â†’| Infinite Canvas | Zoomable timeline interface spanning 12 years |
   188â†’| Semantic Zoom | Data representation changes based on zoom level |
   189â†’| Bill of Materials | List of parts required for a specific PM type |
   190â†’| Material Reservation | Soft or hard allocation of inventory to a scheduled PM |
   191â†’| **Unavailability Event** | A corrective maintenance event, incident, or external factor that removes a train from service (source: Maximo CM work orders, incidents, or manual entry) |
   192â†’| **Service Demand** | Minimum number of trains required for passenger service by day type (weekday, Saturday, Sunday, holiday) |
   193â†’| **Utilization Profile** | Expected daily mileage accumulation rate based on route assignment (e.g., NSL = 350 km/day, EWL = 180 km/day) |
   194â†’| **Cold Start** | Scenario where PM history is incomplete during data migration, requiring estimation with confidence levels |
   195â†’| **Track Capability** | Specific equipment or features available on a maintenance track (e.g., pit access, crane, lifting jacks) |
   196â†’| **PM Capability Requirement** | Equipment or track features required to perform a specific PM type |
   197â†’
   198â†’---
   199â†’
   200â†’## 1.8 Document Structure
   201â†’
   202â†’This specification is divided into the following parts:
   203â†’
   204â†’| Part | Title | Audience |
   205â†’|------|-------|----------|
   206â†’| 1 | Executive Summary & Decision Record | All stakeholders |
   207â†’| 2 | System Architecture | Architects, Tech Leads |
   208â†’| 3 | Database Schema | Backend Engineers, DBAs |
   209â†’| 4 | Domain Services | Backend Engineers |
   210â†’| 5 | API Specification | Frontend Engineers, Integrators |
   211â†’| 6 | User Interface Specification | Frontend Engineers, UX Designers |
   212â†’| 7 | Integration Specifications | Integration Team |
   213â†’| 8 | Operations & Deployment | DevOps, SRE |
   214â†’---
   215â†’
   216â†’## 1.9 Critical Dependencies
   217â†’
   218â†’The following external dependencies are **required** for MMS to produce operationally valid schedules:
   219â†’
   220â†’| Dependency | Source | Frequency | Fallback |
   221â†’|------------|--------|-----------|----------|
   222â†’| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | Real-time (or 15-min polling) | Manual status update UI with prominent warning |
   223â†’| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Daily snapshot | Assume unlimited (with warning flag) |
   224â†’| Service demand | Operations input | Initial + updates | Default to 85% fleet availability |
   225â†’| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Initial + updates | Fleet-wide average (with warning flag) |
   226â†’
   227â†’---
   228â†’
   229â†’## 1.10 Change Log
   230â†’
   231â†’| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
   232â†’|---------|------|--------|---------|
   233â†’| 1.0 | 2026-01-01 | Initial | Original specification |
   234â†’| 2.0 | 2026-01-14 | Review | Added: Material constraints, Explanation trace, Baseline/drift architecture, Scenario management, Component tracking, Audit trail, Constraint aggregation layer |
   235â†’| **2.1** | **2026-01-19** | **Gap Analysis** | **Added: ADR-011 through ADR-014 (unavailability, service demand, route utilization, cold start); R-011 through R-014; Scope clarification distinguishing "manages" from "receives"; New glossary terms; Critical dependencies section; Service demand and unavailability awareness in system purpose** |

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 1: Executive Summary & Decision Record

**Document Version:** 2.1  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19

---

## 1.1 System Purpose

The Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS) is a constraint-based fleet management platform that optimizes train deployments and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year contract period. It replaces manual Excel-based planning with an integrated system providing:

1. **Daily mileage tracking** with automated ingestion from CSV uploads
2. **Run assignment optimization** balancing mileage across the fleet
3. **Preventive maintenance scheduling** within resource constraints
4. **Strategic planning** for 1-10 year horizons to prevent "bow wave" accumulation
5. **Man-hour leveling** to optimize workforce utilization
6. **Cost forecasting** with TCO projection and variance analysis
7. **Simulation capabilities** for what-if scenario planning
8. **Unified visualization** via an Infinite Canvas interface
9. **Service demand validation** ensuring fleet availability meets operational requirements
10. **Unavailability awareness** integrating corrective maintenance status for schedule validity

---

## 1.2 Project Scope

### 1.2.1 In Scope (MMS Manages)

| Capability | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| Fleet roster management | Trains, cars, components with lifecycle tracking |
| Preventive Maintenance scheduling | C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul planning with constraint validation |
| Run assignment optimization | Daily route allocation with mileage balancing |
| Mileage projection and tracking | Route-based utilization profiles, 12-year forecasting |
| Constraint validation | Track capacity, labor, materials, unavailability |
| Maximo integration | Bidirectional sync for PM work orders |
| Multi-year capital planning | Bow-wave detection, cost smoothing |
| Service demand validation | Ensuring minimum fleet availability targets |

### 1.2.2 In Scope (MMS Must Receive/React To)

| External Data | Source | Purpose |
|---------------|--------|---------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook/polling | Block unavailable trains from scheduling |
| Inventory snapshots | ERP/Maximo | Validate material constraints |
| Service demand requirements | Operations team | Set minimum availability targets |

### 1.2.3 Out of Scope (MMS Does Not Execute)

| Capability | Owner System | MMS Interaction |
|------------|--------------|-----------------|
| Corrective maintenance work orders | Maximo CMMS | Receives status updates only |
| Parts inventory management | ERP/Maximo | Consumes snapshots for constraint validation |
| Operator scheduling | Workforce management | None |
| Real-time GPS tracking | OCC systems | None |

> **Critical Clarification:** "Out of Scope" means MMS does not *manage* these functions. However, MMS **must receive** corrective maintenance status to prevent scheduling conflicts. Without this visibility, every PM schedule is potentially invalid on the day of execution.

---

## 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions

| ID | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered | Risk Mitigation |
|----|----------|-----------|------------------------|-----------------|
| ADR-001 | TypeScript-First Architecture | Team expertise, single language for 98% of codebase, strong typing | Java (enterprise), Python (data science) | Node.js fallback path documented |
| ADR-002 | C++ Native Binary for Tactical Optimization | Container density (20-30 pods/node), <5ms cold start, OR-Tools native performance | WASM solver (limited), Pure TS (10x slower) | TypeScript fallback solver, Docker build pipeline |
| ADR-003 | Dual-Horizon Planning (Strategic + Tactical) | RFQ requires 1-10 year planning; single solver cannot handle both granularities | Single solver (bow wave risk), Manual strategic planning | Constraint aggregation layer ensures consistency |
| ADR-004 | Soft Constraints with Explainability | Operations cannot accept "INFEASIBLE"; must provide actionable least-bad solutions | Hard constraints only (operationally unacceptable) | Fallback greedy solver, explanation trace |
| ADR-005 | Material Constraints in Optimizer | PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless | Labor-only optimization (cited in reviews as critical gap) | Inventory snapshot integration, reservation system |
| ADR-006 | Baseline/Drift Architecture | Long-term plans cannot recalculate daily; planners need stable reference points | Continuous recalculation (jittery UX, trust erosion) | Published baselines, drift thresholds, dampening |
| ADR-007 | Scenario-Based Simulation | RFQ requires what-if analysis; cannot store in single-reality schema | In-memory only (data loss), Full DB clone (storage bloat) | Event-sourced deltas, materialized comparison views |
| ADR-008 | Infinite Canvas UI (deck.gl) | Semantic zoom from 12-year strategy to daily operations without context switching | Paginated tables (poor UX), Separate views (fragmented) | Progressive enhancement, tabular fallback |
| ADR-009 | Explanation Trace in Optimizer Output | Operators must understand "why" to trust and adopt system recommendations | Black-box output (user rejection, manual overrides) | Factor-based explanations, human-readable descriptions |
| ADR-010 | Audit Trail for All Schedule Changes | 12-year government contract requires full attribution and compliance evidence | Implicit logging only | Immutable history tables, user attribution |
| **ADR-011** | **Unavailability Event Integration** | PM scheduling requires knowledge of trains blocked by corrective work; without this, schedules are invalid on day of execution | Ignore corrective (operationally invalid) | Maximo webhook integration; manual status update UI as fallback |
| **ADR-012** | **Service Demand as Hard Constraint** | Optimizer must know minimum fleet availability target; cannot validate bow-wave risk without knowing "how many trains do we need?" | Assume 100% availability (unrealistic) | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown |
| **ADR-013** | **Route-Based Utilization Profiles** | Mileage projection accuracy requires route-specific daily rates, not fleet averages; projection error compounds over 12-year horizon | Fleet-wide average (60,000 km error over 12 years) | Route utilization profiles table; manual override per train; explicit flags for unknown routes |
| **ADR-014** | **PM History Assumptions for Cold Start** | Data migration must handle incomplete historical records with explicit confidence levels; system must not crash or produce invalid projections from missing data | Assume complete history (crash risk), Ignore missing (false alerts) | Assumption tracking table; inference rules; confidence scoring; manual review queue |

---

## 1.4 Risk Assessment & Mitigation

| Risk ID | Risk Description | Probability | Impact | Mitigation Strategy | Owner |
|---------|------------------|-------------|--------|---------------------|-------|
| R-001 | C++ binary build complexity | Medium | High | Containerized build pipeline, pre-built binaries, TypeScript fallback | DevOps |
| R-002 | Team TypeScript focus with C++ component | Low | Medium | C++ contractor with knowledge transfer, comprehensive integration tests | Tech Lead |
| R-003 | Strategic/Tactical solver inconsistency | Medium | High | Constraint aggregation layer, cross-validation tests, unified constraint model | Architect |
| R-004 | Material constraint data quality | High | High | Inventory snapshot validation, fallback to labor-only mode, data quality dashboard | Data Team |
| R-005 | Operator distrust of optimizer | Medium | High | Explanation traces, gradual rollout, manual override capability, training program | Product |
| R-006 | Long-term plan instability (jitter) | Medium | Medium | Baseline snapshots, drift thresholds, dampening in projections | Backend |
| R-007 | deck.gl WebGL performance on low-end devices | Medium | Medium | Progressive enhancement, LOD rendering, tabular fallback | Frontend |
| R-008 | Data migration quality issues | Medium | High | Parallel-run validation, automated reconciliation, rollback procedures | Data Team |
| R-009 | Scenario storage scalability | Low | Medium | Event-sourced deltas vs. full clones, automatic scenario expiration | Backend |
| R-010 | Maximo integration delays | Medium | Medium | CSV interim solution, shadow record system, dedicated integration team | Integration |
| **R-011** | **No corrective maintenance visibility** | **High** | **Critical** | Unavailability event feed from Maximo; fallback to manual status update UI; alert when train scheduled without availability check | Integration |
| **R-012** | **Service demand undefined** | **High** | **Critical** | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown; prominent warning in UI when using defaults | Product |
| **R-013** | **PM history incomplete at migration** | **High** | **High** | Assumption tracking table (`pm_baseline_assumptions`); inference rules from higher-level PMs; confidence scoring; manual review queue for LOW confidence trains | Data Team |
| **R-014** | **Route utilization unknown** | **Medium** | **High** | Default to fleet-wide average with explicit warning flag; allow manual override per train; route profile editor in admin UI | Product |

---

## 1.7 Glossary of Terms

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| PM | Preventive Maintenance (C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul) |
| Fixed Parameters | Resource constraints (tracks, man-hours) that bound optimization |
| Run Assignment | Allocation of a specific train to a specific route for a day |
| Mileage Leveling | Balancing cumulative mileage across the fleet to equalize wear |
| Man-hour Leveling | Balancing workforce allocation across time periods |
| Compliance Window | Acceptable date range for scheduling a PM (earliest to latest) |
| Bow Wave Risk | Accumulation of deferred maintenance causing future overload |
| Soft Constraint | Optimization constraint that can be violated with penalty cost |
| Hard Constraint | Constraint that must never be violated (safety, physical limits) |
| Strategic Horizon | Long-term planning window (1-10 years, quarterly granularity) |
| Tactical Horizon | Medium-term planning window (12 weeks, daily granularity) |
| Baseline | Published snapshot of a plan used as reference for drift analysis |
| Drift | Deviation between actual state and baseline projection |
| Scenario | Alternative version of a plan for what-if analysis |
| Explanation Trace | Human-readable justification for optimizer decisions |
| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership (12-year projected costs) |
| Infinite Canvas | Zoomable timeline interface spanning 12 years |
| Semantic Zoom | Data representation changes based on zoom level |
| Bill of Materials | List of parts required for a specific PM type |
| Material Reservation | Soft or hard allocation of inventory to a scheduled PM |
| **Unavailability Event** | A corrective maintenance event, incident, or external factor that removes a train from service (source: Maximo CM work orders, incidents, or manual entry) |
| **Service Demand** | Minimum number of trains required for passenger service by day type (weekday, Saturday, Sunday, holiday) |
| **Utilization Profile** | Expected daily mileage accumulation rate based on route assignment (e.g., NSL = 350 km/day, EWL = 180 km/day) |
| **Cold Start** | Scenario where PM history is incomplete during data migration, requiring estimation with confidence levels |
| **Track Capability** | Specific equipment or features available on a maintenance track (e.g., pit access, crane, lifting jacks) |
| **PM Capability Requirement** | Equipment or track features required to perform a specific PM type |

---

## 1.8 Document Structure

This specification is divided into the following parts:

| Part | Title | Audience |
|------|-------|----------|
| 1 | Executive Summary & Decision Record | All stakeholders |
| 2 | System Architecture | Architects, Tech Leads |
| 3 | Database Schema | Backend Engineers, DBAs |
| 4 | Domain Services | Backend Engineers |
| 5 | API Specification | Frontend Engineers, Integrators |
| 6 | User Interface Specification | Frontend Engineers, UX Designers |
| 7 | Integration Specifications | Integration Team |
| 8 | Operations & Deployment | DevOps, SRE |
---

## 1.9 Critical Dependencies

The following external dependencies are **required** for MMS to produce operationally valid schedules:

| Dependency | Source | Frequency | Fallback |
|------------|--------|-----------|----------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | Real-time (or 15-min polling) | Manual status update UI with prominent warning |
| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Daily snapshot | Assume unlimited (with warning flag) |
| Service demand | Operations input | Initial + updates | Default to 85% fleet availability |
| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Initial + updates | Fleet-wide average (with warning flag) |

---

## 1.10 Change Log

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-01-01 | Initial | Original specification |
| 2.0 | 2026-01-14 | Review | Added: Material constraints, Explanation trace, Baseline/drift architecture, Scenario management, Component tracking, Audit trail, Constraint aggregation layer |
| **2.1** | **2026-01-19** | **Gap Analysis** | **Added: ADR-011 through ADR-014 (unavailability, service demand, route utilization, cold start); R-011 through R-014; Scope clarification distinguishing "manages" from "receives"; New glossary terms; Critical dependencies section; Service demand and unavailability awareness in system purpose** |

    47â†’## 7.2 Integration Phasing

    62â†’
    63â†’## 7.3 Mileage Data Ingestion
    64â†’
    65â†’### 7.3.1 CSV File Format
    66â†’
    67â†’**Filename Pattern:** `mileage_YYYYMMDD.csv`  
    68â†’**Encoding:** UTF-8  
    69â†’**Delimiter:** Comma  
    70â†’**Header:** Required
    71â†’
    72â†’```csv
    73â†’train_number,reading_date,odometer_km,source
    74â†’1001,2026-01-14,245152,ATS
    75â†’1002,2026-01-14,248523,ATS
    76â†’1003,2026-01-14,242087,ATS
    77â†’```
    78â†’
    79â†’### 7.3.2 Field Specifications
    80â†’
    81â†’| Field | Type | Required | Validation |
    82â†’|-------|------|----------|------------|
    83â†’| `train_number` | string | Yes | Must exist in trains table |
    84â†’| `reading_date` | date | Yes | Format: YYYY-MM-DD, not future |
    85â†’| `odometer_km` | integer | Yes | Positive, â‰¤ previous + 2000 |
    86â†’| `source` | string | No | Default: 'CSV' |
    87â†’
    88â†’### 7.3.3 Validation Rules
    89â†’
    90â†’```typescript
    91â†’interface MileageValidationRules {
    92â†’  // Format validation
    93â†’  maxFileSize: '10MB';
    94â†’  requiredColumns: ['train_number', 'reading_date', 'odometer_km'];
    95â†’  dateFormat: 'YYYY-MM-DD';
    96â†’  
    97â†’  // Business validation
    98â†’  maxDailyIncrease: 2000;           // km - anything more is anomaly
    99â†’  maxDailyDecrease: 0;              // Negative not allowed without meter event
   100â†’  duplicateHandling: 'UPDATE';       // UPDATE or REJECT
   101â†’  unknownTrainHandling: 'QUARANTINE'; // QUARANTINE or REJECT_FILE
   102â†’  
   103â†’  // Timing
   104â†’  expectedArrivalWindow: {
   105â†’    start: '05:00',                 // Local time
   106â†’    end: '07:00'
   107â†’  };
   108â†’  alertIfMissingAfter: '08:00';
   109â†’}
   110â†’```
   111â†’
   112â†’### 7.3.4 Error Handling
   113â†’
   114â†’```typescript
   115â†’interface IngestionError {
   116â†’  severity: 'WARNING' | 'ERROR' | 'CRITICAL';
   117â†’  code: string;
   118â†’  message: string;
   119â†’  row?: number;
   120â†’  field?: string;
   121â†’  value?: any;
   122â†’  action: 'SKIP_ROW' | 'QUARANTINE_FILE' | 'ALERT_ADMIN';
   123â†’}
   124â†’
   125â†’const ERROR_CODES = {
   126â†’  // File-level errors
   127â†’  'FILE_001': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'File size exceeds limit' },
   128â†’  'FILE_002': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'Invalid file format' },
   129â†’  'FILE_003': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Missing required columns' },
   130â†’  
   131â†’  // Row-level errors
   132â†’  'ROW_001': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Unknown train number' },
   133â†’  'ROW_002': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Future date not allowed' },
   134â†’  'ROW_003': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Negative daily mileage' },
   135â†’  'ROW_004': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Spike detected (>2000km/day)' },
   136â†’  'ROW_005': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Invalid odometer value' },
   137â†’  
   138â†’  // System errors
   139â†’  'SYS_001': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'Database connection failed' },
   140â†’  'SYS_002': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Transaction rollback' }
   141â†’};
   142â†’```

   164â†’
   165â†’### 7.4.2 Shadow Record Pattern
   166â†’
   167â†’MMS maintains "shadow records" that mirror Maximo work orders, allowing operations to continue if Maximo is unavailable.
   168â†’
   169â†’```typescript
   170â†’interface MaximoShadowRecord {
   171â†’  mmsId: string;                    // MMS primary key
   172â†’  maximoWoId: string | null;        // Maximo work order ID (null if not synced)
   173â†’  syncStatus: 'PENDING' | 'SYNCED' | 'CONFLICT' | 'ERROR';
   174â†’  lastSyncAt: Date | null;
   175â†’  lastSyncError: string | null;
   176â†’  
   177â†’  // MMS data (source of truth for scheduling)
   178â†’  mmsData: {
   179â†’    trainId: string;
   180â†’    pmTypeCode: string;
   181â†’    targetDate: Date;
   182â†’    status: string;
   183â†’  };
   184â†’  
   185â†’  // Maximo data (for reconciliation)
   186â†’  maximoData: {
   187â†’    status: string;
   188â†’    actualStart: Date | null;
   189â†’    actualFinish: Date | null;
   190â†’    actualLabor: number | null;
   191â†’  } | null;
   192â†’}
   193â†’```
   194â†’
   195â†’### 7.4.3 Maximo REST API Endpoints
   196â†’
   197â†’**Base URL:** `https://maximo.metro.example/maximo/oslc`
   198â†’
   199â†’| Operation | Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
   200â†’|-----------|--------|----------|---------|
   201â†’| Get Work Order | GET | `/os/mxwo/{wonum}` | Fetch WO details |
   202â†’| Create Work Order | POST | `/os/mxwo` | Create new WO |
   203â†’| Update Work Order | POST | `/os/mxwo/{wonum}` | Update WO status |
   204â†’| List Work Orders | GET | `/os/mxwo` | Query WOs by filter |
   205â†’| Get Asset | GET | `/os/mxasset/{assetnum}` | Fetch asset (train) |
   206â†’| List Inventory | GET | `/os/mxinventory` | Query inventory |
   207â†’
   208â†’### 7.4.4 Work Order Creation
   209â†’
   210â†’When MMS schedules a PM, it creates a shadow record. On sync:
   211â†’
   212â†’```typescript
   213â†’interface MaximoWorkOrderCreate {
   214â†’  // Required fields
   215â†’  siteid: string;                  // 'METRO'
   216â†’  woclass: string;                 // 'WORKORDER'
   217â†’  worktype: string;                // 'PM'
   218â†’  assetnum: string;                // Train number
   219â†’  description: string;             // PM description
   220â†’  schedstart: string;              // ISO 8601 date
   221â†’  schedfinish: string;             // ISO 8601 date
   222â†’  
   223â†’  // Optional fields
   224â†’  location: string;                // Depot code
   225â†’  estlabhrs: number;               // Estimated hours
   226â†’  pmnum: string;                   // PM type reference
   227â†’  
   228â†’  // Custom fields (metro-specific)
   229â†’  mms_schedule_id: string;         // Link back to MMS
   230â†’  mms_constraint_start: string;    // Compliance window start
   231â†’  mms_constraint_end: string;      // Compliance window end
   232â†’}
   233â†’
   234â†’// Example
   235â†’const createWorkOrder = async (pm: PMSchedule): Promise<string> => {
   236â†’  const payload: MaximoWorkOrderCreate = {
   237â†’    siteid: 'METRO',
   238â†’    woclass: 'WORKORDER',
   239â†’    worktype: 'PM',
   240â†’    assetnum: pm.train.trainNumber,
   241â†’    description: `${pm.pmType.pmCode} - ${pm.pmType.pmName}`,
   242â†’    schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
   243â†’    schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString(),
   244â†’    location: pm.assignedDepot.code,
   245â†’    estlabhrs: pm.assignedHours,
   246â†’    pmnum: pm.pmType.pmCode,
   247â†’    mms_schedule_id: pm.id,
   248â†’    mms_constraint_start: pm.constraintStart.toISOString(),
   249â†’    mms_constraint_end: pm.constraintEnd.toISOString()
   250â†’  };
   251â†’  
   252â†’  const response = await maximoClient.post('/os/mxwo', payload);
   253â†’  return response.data.wonum;
   254â†’};
   255â†’```
   256â†’
   257â†’### 7.4.5 Work Order Status Mapping
   258â†’
   259â†’| Maximo Status | MMS Status | Sync Action | MMS Edit Lock |
   260â†’|---------------|------------|-------------|---------------|
   261â†’| WAPPR | SCHEDULED | None (initial state) | â‌Œ Unlocked |
   262â†’| APPR | SCHEDULED | None | â‌Œ Unlocked |
   263â†’| INPRG | IN_PROGRESS | Update MMS status | âœ… **LOCKED** |
   264â†’| COMP | COMPLETED | Update MMS, record actuals | âœ… **LOCKED** |
   265â†’| CLOSE | COMPLETED | Final state | âœ… **LOCKED** |
   266â†’| CAN | CANCELLED | Update MMS status | âœ… **LOCKED** |
   267â†’
   268â†’### 7.4.6 Status-Based Edit Locking
   269â†’
   270â†’**CRITICAL:** When a Maximo work order is in progress (INPRG), the corresponding PM record in MMS must be **read-only**. This prevents race conditions where a planner reschedules a PM that technicians are actively working on.
   271â†’
   272â†’```typescript
   273â†’/**
   274â†’ * Validate PM edit request against Maximo status.
   275â†’ * MUST be called before any PM schedule modification.
   276â†’ * 
   277â†’ * This addresses the race condition where:
   278â†’ * 1. Technician opens work order on tablet (Maximo status = INPRG)
   279â†’ * 2. Planner reschedules PM in MMS (unaware of in-progress work)
   280â†’ * 3. Maximo sync fails or overwrites technician's work
   281â†’ * 
   282â†’ * Operational reality MUST override planning data.
   283â†’ */
   284â†’class PMEditValidator {
   285â†’  async validateEdit(pmId: string): Promise<EditValidationResult> {
   286â†’    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
   287â†’    
   288â†’    // Check 1: Maximo status lock
   289â†’    if (pm.shadowRecord?.maximoData?.status) {
   290â†’      const lockedStatuses = ['INPRG', 'COMP', 'CLOSE', 'CAN'];
   291â†’      
   292â†’      if (lockedStatuses.includes(pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status)) {
   293â†’        return {
   294â†’          valid: false,
   295â†’          reason: 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK',
   296â†’          message: `Cannot modify: Work order is ${this.formatStatus(pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status)} in Maximo`,
   297â†’          maximoStatus: pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status,
   298â†’          maximoWonum: pm.maximoWoId,
   299â†’          suggestion: pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status === 'INPRG' 
   300â†’            ? 'Wait for technicians to complete work, or contact supervisor to cancel in Maximo first'
   301â†’            : 'This PM has been completed/cancelled. Create a new PM if rescheduling is needed.'
   302â†’        };
   303â†’      }
   304â†’    }
   305â†’    
   306â†’    // Check 2: Pending sync conflict
   307â†’    if (pm.shadowRecord?.syncStatus === 'CONFLICT') {
   308â†’      return {
   309â†’        valid: false,
   310â†’        reason: 'SYNC_CONFLICT',
   311â†’        message: 'Unresolved sync conflict exists. Resolve before editing.',
   312â†’        suggestion: 'Go to Integration > Conflicts to resolve'
   313â†’      };
   314â†’    }
   315â†’    
   316â†’    // Check 3: Baseline lock (optional, if locked in current baseline)
   317â†’    if (pm.lockedInBaseline) {
   318â†’      return {
   319â†’        valid: false,
   320â†’        reason: 'BASELINE_LOCK',
   321â†’        message: 'PM is locked in published baseline',
   322â†’        suggestion: 'Unlock in baseline editor or create scenario to test changes'
   323â†’      };
   324â†’    }
   325â†’    
   326â†’    return { valid: true };
   327â†’  }
   328â†’  
   329â†’  private formatStatus(status: string): string {
   330â†’    const statusLabels: Record<string, string> = {
   331â†’      'INPRG': 'In Progress',
   332â†’      'COMP': 'Completed',
   333â†’      'CLOSE': 'Closed',
   334â†’      'CAN': 'Cancelled'
   335â†’    };
   336â†’    return statusLabels[status] || status;
   337â†’  }
   338â†’}
   339â†’
   340â†’interface EditValidationResult {
   341â†’  valid: boolean;
   342â†’  reason?: 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK' | 'SYNC_CONFLICT' | 'BASELINE_LOCK';
   343â†’  message?: string;
   344â†’  maximoStatus?: string;
   345â†’  maximoWonum?: string;
   346â†’  suggestion?: string;
   347â†’}
   348â†’```
   349â†’
   350â†’**API Integration:**
   351â†’
   352â†’All PM modification endpoints MUST call this validator:
   353â†’
   354â†’```typescript
   355â†’// In PM Schedule API (Part 5)
   356â†’app.patch('/api/v1/pm-schedule/:id', async (req, res) => {
   357â†’  const validation = await pmEditValidator.validateEdit(req.params.id);
   358â†’  
   359â†’  if (!validation.valid) {
   360â†’    return res.status(423).json({  // 423 Locked
   361â†’      error: validation.reason,
   362â†’      message: validation.message,
   363â†’      suggestion: validation.suggestion,
   364â†’      maximoStatus: validation.maximoStatus
   365â†’    });
   366â†’  }
   367â†’  
   368â†’  // Proceed with update...
   369â†’});
   370â†’```
   371â†’
   372â†’**Canvas UI Integration:**

   374â†’When displaying PM blocks, check lock status and show visual indicator:
   375â†’
   376â†’```typescript
   377â†’// In Canvas layer (Part 6)
   378â†’function getPMBlockStyle(pm: PMSchedule): PMBlockStyle {
   379â†’  if (pm.shadowRecord?.maximoData?.status === 'INPRG') {
   380â†’    return {
   381â†’      cursor: 'not-allowed',
   382â†’      opacity: 0.7,
   383â†’      border: '2px dashed #F59E0B',  // Amber dashed = in progress
   384â†’      draggable: false,
   385â†’      tooltip: 'In Progress in Maximo - cannot reschedule'
   386â†’    };
   387â†’  }
   388â†’  // ... normal styling
   389â†’}
   390â†’```
   391â†’
   392â†’### 7.4.7 Bidirectional Sync Logic
   393â†’
   394â†’```typescript
   395â†’class MaximoSyncService {
   396â†’  /**
   397â†’   * Push MMS changes to Maximo
   398â†’   */
   399â†’  async pushToMaximo(pmId: string): Promise<SyncResult> {
   400â†’    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
   401â†’    
   402â†’    if (!pm.maximoWoId) {
   403â†’      // Create new work order
   404â†’      const wonum = await this.createWorkOrder(pm);
   405â†’      await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
   406â†’        maximoWoId: wonum, 
   407â†’        syncStatus: 'SYNCED' 
   408â†’      });
   409â†’      return { action: 'CREATED', wonum };
   410â†’    }
   411â†’    
   412â†’    // Update existing work order
   413â†’    const maximoWO = await this.getMaximoWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId);
   414â†’    
   415â†’    if (maximoWO.status === 'INPRG' || maximoWO.status === 'COMP') {
   416â†’      // Cannot update in-progress or completed WOs
   417â†’      return { action: 'SKIPPED', reason: 'WO already in progress' };
   418â†’    }
   419â†’    
   420â†’    // Check for conflicts
   421â†’    if (new Date(maximoWO.changedate) > pm.shadowRecord.lastSyncAt) {
   422â†’      await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { syncStatus: 'CONFLICT' });
   423â†’      return { action: 'CONFLICT', maximoData: maximoWO };
   424â†’    }
   425â†’    
   426â†’    // Push update
   427â†’    await this.updateWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId, {
   428â†’      schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
   429â†’      schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString(),
   430â†’      location: pm.assignedDepot.code
   431â†’    });
   432â†’    
   433â†’    await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
   434â†’      syncStatus: 'SYNCED',
   435â†’      lastSyncAt: new Date()
   436â†’    });
   437â†’    
   438â†’    return { action: 'UPDATED', wonum: pm.maximoWoId };
   439â†’  }
   440â†’  
   441â†’  /**
   442â†’   * Pull Maximo changes to MMS
   443â†’   */
   444â†’  async pullFromMaximo(): Promise<PullResult> {
   445â†’    // Get recently modified work orders
   446â†’    const modifiedSince = await this.getLastPullTimestamp();
   447â†’    const workOrders = await this.queryMaximoWorkOrders({
   448â†’      worktype: 'PM',
   449â†’      changedate: `>${modifiedSince.toISOString()}`
   450â†’    });
   451â†’    
   452â†’    const results: PullResult = {
   453â†’      processed: 0,
   454â†’      updated: 0,
   455â†’      conflicts: 0,
   456â†’      errors: 0
   457â†’    };
   458â†’    
   459â†’    for (const wo of workOrders) {
   460â†’      results.processed++;
   461â†’      
   462â†’      try {
   463â†’        const pm = await this.findPMByMaximoId(wo.wonum);
   464â†’        
   465â†’        if (!pm) {
   466â†’          // Work order not managed by MMS
   467â†’          continue;
   468â†’        }
   469â†’        
   470â†’        // Status change
   471â†’        if (wo.status !== pm.shadowRecord.maximoData?.status) {
   472â†’          await this.handleStatusChange(pm, wo);
   473â†’          results.updated++;
   474â†’        }
   475â†’        
   476â†’        // Actuals recorded
   477â†’        if (wo.actlabhrs && !pm.actualHours) {
   478â†’          await this.recordActuals(pm, wo);
   479â†’          results.updated++;
   480â†’        }
   481â†’        
   482â†’      } catch (error) {
   483â†’        results.errors++;
   484â†’        this.logger.error({ wo, error }, 'Pull processing failed');
   485â†’      }
   486â†’    }
   487â†’    
   488â†’    await this.setLastPullTimestamp(new Date());
   489â†’    return results;
   490â†’  }
   491â†’  
   492â†’  /**
   493â†’   * Resolve sync conflict
   494â†’   */
   495â†’  async resolveConflict(
   496â†’    pmId: string, 
   497â†’    resolution: 'USE_MMS' | 'USE_MAXIMO'
   498â†’  ): Promise<void> {
   499â†’    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
   500â†’    
   501â†’    if (resolution === 'USE_MMS') {
   502â†’      // Force push MMS data to Maximo
   503â†’      await this.updateWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId, {
   504â†’        schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
   505â†’        schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString()
   506â†’      }, { force: true });
   507â†’    } else {
   508â†’      // Apply Maximo data to MMS
   509â†’      const maximoWO = await this.getMaximoWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId);
   510â†’      await this.updatePMFromMaximo(pm.id, maximoWO);
   511â†’    }
   512â†’    
   513â†’    await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
   514â†’      syncStatus: 'SYNCED',
   515â†’      lastSyncAt: new Date()
   516â†’    });
   517â†’  }
   518â†’}
   519â†’```

   706â†’## 7.7 Report Generation
   707â†’
   708â†’### 7.7.1 Report Types
   709â†’
   710â†’| Report | Format | Schedule | Description |
   711â†’|--------|--------|----------|-------------|
   712â†’| Weekly Compliance | PDF | Monday 06:00 | PM compliance status by depot |
   713â†’| Monthly TCO | Excel | 1st of month | Cost breakdown by category |
   714â†’| Fleet Mileage | Excel | Daily 08:00 | Mileage readings and anomalies |
   715â†’| Man-hour Projection | PDF | Monday 06:00 | 12-week workload forecast |
   716â†’| Drift Analysis | PDF | Daily 07:00 | Baseline comparison summary |
   717â†’
   718â†’### 7.7.2 Report Generation Service
   719â†’
   720â†’```typescript
   721â†’class ReportGenerationService {
   722â†’  async generateReport(
   723â†’    type: ReportType,
   724â†’    params: ReportParams
   725â†’  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
   726â†’    const template = await this.loadTemplate(type);
   727â†’    const data = await this.fetchReportData(type, params);
   728â†’    
   729â†’    switch (template.format) {
   730â†’      case 'PDF':
   731â†’        return this.generatePDF(template, data);
   732â†’      case 'XLSX':
   733â†’        return this.generateExcel(template, data);
   734â†’      default:
   735â†’        throw new Error(`Unsupported format: ${template.format}`);
   736â†’    }
   737â†’  }
   738â†’  
   739â†’  private async generatePDF(
   740â†’    template: ReportTemplate,
   741â†’    data: any
   742â†’  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
   743â†’    // Use Puppeteer for PDF generation
   744â†’    const html = await this.renderTemplate(template.htmlTemplate, data);
   745â†’    
   746â†’    const browser = await puppeteer.launch({ headless: true });
   747â†’    const page = await browser.newPage();
   748â†’    await page.setContent(html);
   749â†’    
   750â†’    const pdfBuffer = await page.pdf({
   751â†’      format: 'A4',
   752â†’      margin: { top: '1cm', right: '1cm', bottom: '1cm', left: '1cm' },
   753â†’      printBackground: true
   754â†’    });
   755â†’    
   756â†’    await browser.close();
   757â†’    
   758â†’    // Store in MinIO
   759â†’    const filename = `${template.name}_${format(new Date(), 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss')}.pdf`;
   760â†’    const url = await this.storage.upload(filename, pdfBuffer);
   761â†’    
   762â†’    return {
   763â†’      filename,
   764â†’      url,
   765â†’      expiresAt: addDays(new Date(), 7),
   766â†’      sizeBytes: pdfBuffer.length
   767â†’    };
   768â†’  }
   769â†’  
   770â†’  private async generateExcel(
   771â†’    template: ReportTemplate,
   772â†’    data: any
   773â†’  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
   774â†’    // Use ExcelJS for Excel generation
   775â†’    const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
   776â†’    
   777â†’    for (const sheetDef of template.sheets) {
   778â†’      const sheet = workbook.addWorksheet(sheetDef.name);
   779â†’      
   780â†’      // Add headers
   781â†’      sheet.addRow(sheetDef.columns.map(c => c.header));
   782â†’      
   783â†’      // Add data rows
   784â†’      const sheetData = data[sheetDef.dataKey];
   785â†’      for (const row of sheetData) {
   786â†’        sheet.addRow(sheetDef.columns.map(c => row[c.key]));
   787â†’      }
   788â†’      
   789â†’      // Apply formatting
   790â†’      this.applyExcelFormatting(sheet, sheetDef);
   791â†’    }
   792â†’    
   793â†’    const buffer = await workbook.xlsx.writeBuffer();
   794â†’    
   795â†’    const filename = `${template.name}_${format(new Date(), 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss')}.xlsx`;
   796â†’    const url = await this.storage.upload(filename, buffer);
   797â†’    
   798â†’    return {
   799â†’      filename,
   800â†’      url,
   801â†’      expiresAt: addDays(new Date(), 7),
   802â†’      sizeBytes: buffer.length
   803â†’    };
   804â†’  }
   805â†’}
   806â†’```

   947â†’interface RetryConfig {
   948â†’  maxAttempts: number;
   949â†’  initialDelayMs: number;

   951â†’  backoffMultiplier: number;
   952â†’  retryableErrors: string[];
   953â†’}
   954â†’
   955â†’const INTEGRATION_RETRY_CONFIGS: Record<string, RetryConfig> = {
   956â†’  maximo: {

   959â†’    maxDelayMs: 60000,
   960â†’    backoffMultiplier: 2,
   961â†’    retryableErrors: ['ECONNREFUSED', 'ETIMEDOUT', '503', '504']
   962â†’  },
   963â†’  email: {

   965â†’    initialDelayMs: 5000,
   966â†’    maxDelayMs: 30000,
   967â†’    backoffMultiplier: 2,
   968â†’    retryableErrors: ['ECONNREFUSED', 'ETIMEDOUT']
   969â†’  },
   970â†’  fileIngestion: {

   972â†’    initialDelayMs: 1000,
   973â†’    maxDelayMs: 10000,

   976â†’  }
   977â†’};
   978â†’```
   979â†’
   980â†’### 7.10.2 Circuit Breaker
   981â†’
   982â†’```typescript
   983â†’interface CircuitBreakerConfig {
   984â†’  failureThreshold: number;    // Failures before opening
   985â†’  successThreshold: number;    // Successes before closing
   986â†’  timeout: number;             // Time in open state before half-open (ms)
   987â†’}
   988â†’
   989â†’class CircuitBreaker {
   990â†’  private state: 'CLOSED' | 'OPEN' | 'HALF_OPEN' = 'CLOSED';
   991â†’  private failures = 0;
   992â†’  private successes = 0;
   993â†’  private lastFailureTime: Date | null = null;
   994â†’  
   995â†’  async execute<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
   996â†’    if (this.state === 'OPEN') {
   997â†’      if (Date.now() - this.lastFailureTime!.getTime() > this.config.timeout) {
   998â†’        this.state = 'HALF_OPEN';
   999â†’      } else {
  1000â†’        throw new Error('Circuit breaker is OPEN');
  1001â†’      }
  1002â†’    }
  1003â†’    
  1004â†’    try {
  1005â†’      const result = await fn();
  1006â†’      this.onSuccess();
  1007â†’      return result;
  1008â†’    } catch (error) {
  1009â†’      this.onFailure();
  1010â†’      throw error;
  1011â†’    }
  1012â†’  }
  1013â†’  
  1014â†’  private onSuccess() {
  1015â†’    this.failures = 0;
  1016â†’    if (this.state === 'HALF_OPEN') {
  1017â†’      this.successes++;
  1018â†’      if (this.successes >= this.config.successThreshold) {
  1019â†’        this.state = 'CLOSED';
  1020â†’        this.successes = 0;
  1021â†’      }
  1022â†’    }
  1023â†’  }
  1024â†’  
  1025â†’  private onFailure() {
  1026â†’    this.failures++;
  1027â†’    this.lastFailureTime = new Date();
  1028â†’    if (this.failures >= this.config.failureThreshold) {
  1029â†’      this.state = 'OPEN';
  1030â†’    }
  1031â†’  }
  1032â†’}
  1033â†’```

   161â†’## 2.3 Critical Data Flows
   162â†’
   163â†’### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow
   164â†’
   165â†’```
   166â†’06:00 â”‚ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) â†’ File Watcher detects
   167â†’06:05 â”‚ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
   168â†’06:10 â”‚ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
   169â†’06:15 â”‚ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
   170â†’06:30 â”‚ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
   171â†’      â”‚   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
   172â†’      â”‚   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
   173â†’07:00 â”‚ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
   174â†’07:00 â”‚ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
   175â†’      â”‚   - View explanation factors for each assignment
   176â†’      â”‚   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
   177â†’08:00 â”‚ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
   178â†’16:00 â”‚ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
   179â†’22:00 â”‚ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
   180â†’```
   181â†’
   182â†’### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow
   183â†’
   184â†’```
   185â†’Monday 06:00  â”‚ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
   186â†’              â”‚   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
   187â†’              â”‚   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
   188â†’Monday 06:30  â”‚ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
   189â†’              â”‚   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
   190â†’              â”‚   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
   191â†’Monday 07:00  â”‚ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
   192â†’              â”‚   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
   193â†’              â”‚   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
   194â†’Monday 08:00  â”‚ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
   195â†’Tuesday       â”‚ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
   196â†’              â”‚   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
   197â†’              â”‚   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
   198â†’Wednesday    â”‚ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
   199â†’Friday       â”‚ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
   200â†’```
   201â†’
   202â†’### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow
   203â†’
   204â†’```
   205â†’User Action  â”‚ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
   206â†’             â”‚   - System validates: no critical conflicts
   207â†’             â”‚   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
   208â†’API Call     â”‚ POST /api/v1/baselines
   209â†’             â”‚   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
   210â†’             â”‚   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
   211â†’             â”‚   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
   212â†’             â”‚   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
   213â†’Post-Publish â”‚ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
   214â†’             â”‚ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
   215â†’```
   216â†’
   217â†’### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow
   218â†’
   219â†’```
   220â†’User Action  â”‚ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
   221â†’             â”‚   - System forks from current published baseline
   222â†’             â”‚   - New scenario_id assigned
   223â†’Editing      â”‚ Planner makes changes within scenario
   224â†’             â”‚   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
   225â†’             â”‚   - Original baseline data unchanged
   226â†’Comparison   â”‚ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
   227â†’             â”‚   - System materializes both scenarios
   228â†’             â”‚   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
   229â†’             â”‚   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
   230â†’Promotion    â”‚ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
   231â†’             â”‚   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
   232â†’             â”‚   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
   233â†’```

   433â†’
   434â†’### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol
   435â†’
   436â†’**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.
   437â†’
   438â†’```typescript
   439â†’// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
   440â†’class OptimizerWorker {
   441â†’  private process: ChildProcess;
   442â†’  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
   443â†’  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
   444â†’  
   445â†’  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
   446â†’  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
   447â†’  
   448â†’  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
   449â†’    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   450â†’      // Start heartbeat monitor
   451â†’      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
   452â†’      
   453â†’      // Handle stream events
   454â†’      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
   455â†’        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
   456â†’          if (!line.trim()) continue;
   457â†’          
   458â†’          try {
   459â†’            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
   460â†’            
   461â†’            switch (msg.type) {
   462â†’              case 'HEARTBEAT':
   463â†’                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   464â†’                break;
   465â†’                
   466â†’              case 'PROGRESS':
   467â†’                this.emitProgress(msg);
   468â†’                break;
   469â†’                
   470â†’              case 'RESULT':
   471â†’                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   472â†’                resolve(msg);
   473â†’                break;
   474â†’                
   475â†’              case 'ERROR':
   476â†’                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   477â†’                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
   478â†’                break;
   479â†’            }
   480â†’          } catch (parseError) {
   481â†’            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
   482â†’          }
   483â†’        }
   484â†’      });
   485â†’      
   486â†’      // Handle process crash
   487â†’      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
   488â†’        if (code !== 0) {
   489â†’          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   490â†’          reject(new OptimizerError({
   491â†’            type: 'ERROR',
   492â†’            request_id: request.request_id,
   493â†’            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
   494â†’            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
   495â†’            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
   496â†’          }));
   497â†’        }
   498â†’      });
   499â†’      
   500â†’      // Send request
   501â†’      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
   502â†’    });
   503â†’  }
   504â†’  
   505â†’  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
   506â†’    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   507â†’    
   508â†’    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
   509â†’      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
   510â†’      
   511â†’      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
   512â†’        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   513â†’        
   514â†’        // Kill hung process
   515â†’        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
   516â†’        
   517â†’        reject(new OptimizerError({
   518â†’          type: 'ERROR',
   519â†’          request_id: requestId,
   520â†’          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
   521â†’          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
   522â†’          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
   523â†’        }));
   524â†’      }
   525â†’    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
   526â†’  }
   527â†’  
   528â†’  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
   529â†’    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
   530â†’      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
   531â†’      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
   532â†’    }
   533â†’  }
   534â†’}
   535â†’
   536â†’class OptimizerError extends Error {
   537â†’  readonly errorCode: string;
   538â†’  readonly requestId: string;
   539â†’  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
   540â†’  readonly stackTrace?: string;
   541â†’  
   542â†’  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
   543â†’    super(msg.error_message);
   544â†’    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
   545â†’    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
   546â†’    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
   547â†’    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
   548â†’    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
   549â†’  }
   550â†’}
   551â†’```
   552â†’
   553â†’**C++ Implementation Notes:**
   554â†’
   555â†’The C++ binary must:
   556â†’1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
   557â†’2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
   558â†’3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
   559â†’4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)
   560â†’
   561â†’```cpp
   562â†’// Signal handler example (C++)
   563â†’void signalHandler(int signal) {
   564â†’    nlohmann::json error;
   565â†’    error["type"] = "ERROR";
   566â†’    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
   567â†’    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
   568â†’    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
   569â†’    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
   570â†’    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
   571â†’    
   572â†’    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
   573â†’    std::cout.flush();
   574â†’    
   575â†’    _exit(128 + signal);
   576â†’}
   577â†’```
   578â†’
   579â†’The Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.
   580â†’
   581â†’### 2.5.1 Purpose
   582â†’
   583â†’The Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.
   584â†’
   585â†’### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process
   586â†’
   587â†’```typescript
   588â†’interface ConstraintEnvelope {
   589â†’  period: {
   590â†’    start: Date;  // Quarter start
   591â†’    end: Date;    // Quarter end
   592â†’  };
   593â†’  
   594â†’  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
   595â†’  track_capacity: {
   596â†’    depot_id: string;
   597â†’    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
   598â†’    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
   599â†’    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
   600â†’    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
   601â†’  }[];
   602â†’  
   603â†’  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
   604â†’  labor_capacity: {
   605â†’    depot_id: string;
   606â†’    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
   607â†’    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
   608â†’    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
   609â†’    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
   610â†’  }[];
   611â†’  
   612â†’  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
   613â†’  material_capacity: {
   614â†’    depot_id: string;
   615â†’    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
   616â†’    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
   617â†’    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
   618â†’    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
   619â†’  }[];
   620â†’  
   621â†’  // Constraints from tactical model
   622â†’  blocked_trains: {
   623â†’    train_id: string;
   624â†’    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
   625â†’    reason: string;
   626â†’  }[];
   627â†’}
   628â†’
   629â†’class ConstraintAggregationService {
   630â†’  /**
   631â†’   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   632â†’   */
   633â†’  async generateEnvelope(
   634â†’    quarterStart: Date,
   635â†’    quarterEnd: Date,
   636â†’    depotIds: string[]
   637â†’  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
   638â†’    // 1. Aggregate track availability
   639â†’    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
   640â†’      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   641â†’    );
   642â†’    
   643â†’    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
   644â†’    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
   645â†’      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   646â†’    );
   647â†’    
   648â†’    // 3. Aggregate material projections
   649â†’    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
   650â†’      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   651â†’    );
   652â†’    
   653â†’    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
   654â†’    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
   655â†’      quarterStart, quarterEnd
   656â†’    );
   657â†’    
   658â†’    return {
   659â†’      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
   660â†’      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
   661â†’      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
   662â†’      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
   663â†’      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
   664â†’    };
   665â†’  }
   666â†’  
   667â†’  /**
   668â†’   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   669â†’   */
   670â†’  async validateStrategicAllocation(
   671â†’    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
   672â†’    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
   673â†’  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
   674â†’    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
   675â†’    
   676â†’    // Check track capacity
   677â†’    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
   678â†’      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
   679â†’        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
   680â†’             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
   681â†’      );
   682â†’      
   683â†’      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
   684â†’        violations.push({
   685â†’          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
   686â†’          severity: 'HARD',
   687â†’          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
   688â†’        });
   689â†’      }
   690â†’    }
   691â†’    
   692â†’    // Check labor capacity
   693â†’    // Check material capacity
   694â†’    // ... similar validation logic
   695â†’    
   696â†’    return {
   697â†’      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
   698â†’      violations
   699â†’    };
   700â†’  }
   701â†’}
   702â†’```
   703â†’
   704â†’---
   705â†’
   706â†’## 2.6 Key Design Principles
   707â†’
   708â†’### 2.6.1 Explainability First
   709â†’
   710â†’Every optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:
   711â†’
   712â†’- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
   713â†’- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
   714â†’- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
   715â†’- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used
   716â†’
   717â†’### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation
   718â†’
   719â†’The system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:
   720â†’
   721â†’- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
   722â†’- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
   723â†’- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
   724â†’- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records
   725â†’
   726â†’### 2.6.3 Audit Everything
   727â†’
   728â†’For 12-year government contract compliance:
   729â†’
   730â†’- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
   731â†’- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
   732â†’- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
   733â†’- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved
   734â†’
   735â†’### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning
   736â†’
   737â†’Prevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:
   738â†’
   739â†’- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
   740â†’- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
   741â†’- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
   742â†’- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
   743â†’

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

## 2.2 Component Responsibilities

### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |

### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |

### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |

### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers

| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
|--------|----------------|-------------|
| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |

### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |

### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow

```
06:00 â”‚ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) â†’ File Watcher detects
06:05 â”‚ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
06:10 â”‚ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
06:15 â”‚ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
06:30 â”‚ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
      â”‚   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
      â”‚   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
07:00 â”‚ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
07:00 â”‚ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
      â”‚   - View explanation factors for each assignment
      â”‚   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
08:00 â”‚ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
16:00 â”‚ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
22:00 â”‚ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
```

### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow

```
Monday 06:00  â”‚ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
              â”‚   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
              â”‚   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
Monday 06:30  â”‚ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
              â”‚   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
              â”‚   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
Monday 07:00  â”‚ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
              â”‚   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
              â”‚   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
Monday 08:00  â”‚ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
Tuesday       â”‚ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
              â”‚   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
              â”‚   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
Wednesday    â”‚ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
Friday       â”‚ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
```

### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow

```
User Action  â”‚ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
             â”‚   - System validates: no critical conflicts
             â”‚   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
API Call     â”‚ POST /api/v1/baselines
             â”‚   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
             â”‚   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
             â”‚   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
             â”‚   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
Post-Publish â”‚ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
             â”‚ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
```

### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow

```
User Action  â”‚ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
             â”‚   - System forks from current published baseline
             â”‚   - New scenario_id assigned
Editing      â”‚ Planner makes changes within scenario
             â”‚   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
             â”‚   - Original baseline data unchanged
Comparison   â”‚ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
             â”‚   - System materializes both scenarios
             â”‚   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
             â”‚   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
Promotion    â”‚ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
             â”‚   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
             â”‚   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
```
